extra.reasoning: File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts_blind/item-d9b37d4e9540.txt. The property requires not (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 (priority 50), whose precondition is the conjunction of 8 booleans INCLUDING renal_impairment. rec_drug_b is set true only by r5 (priority 50), whose precondition is exactly renal_impairment. Since r1's firing condition entails renal_impairment, any input that fires r1 also necessarily fires r5. No higher-priority or competing rule exists for either output (each output has a single setter rule; the default only applies when unset), so neither true value can be suppressed by conflict resolution. Therefore setting renal_impairment, g6pd_deficiency, heart_failure, immunosuppressed, seizure_history, qt_prolongation, peptic_ulcer, and bradycardia all true makes r1 fire (rec_drug_a=true) and r5 fire (rec_drug_b=true) simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The remaining inputs (anticoagulated, elderly, pregnancy) are irrelevant; set to false. Property is violated.
